creator: blackend@FreeBSD.org
text:
On Fri, Aug 06, 2010 at 08:00:11AM +0000, Johann Kois wrote:
> The following reply was made to PR docs/149230; it has been noted by GNATS.
> 
> From: Johann Kois <jkois@FreeBSD.org>
> To: bug-followup@FreeBSD.org, nobody99@gmx.at
> Cc:  
> Subject: Re: docs/149230: [handbook] Homepage is not correct, no boot floppy
>  exist
> Date: Fri, 06 Aug 2010 09:57:54 +0000
> 
>  -----BEGIN PGP SIGNED MESSAGE-----
>  Hash: SHA1
>  
>  Not a website problem.  Rather a problem of the handbook (the original,
>  meaning the English version).
>  
>  Root cause:
>  Automatic build of floppy images has been disabled starting with 8.0.
>  
>  See the following thread on the stable mailinglist for more information:
>  http://lists.freebsd.org/pipermail/freebsd-stable/2010-May/056705.html
>  
>  Best way to proceed for the moment is probably to put a note into the
>  handbook "that you can still create floppy images under 8.0.  But you
>  must do it manually".  As the floppy images are still there for 7.X.
>

If you have time to work on this addition, please do it :)
If not I'll do it with the rest of some updates I'm (and others like
Glen Barber) working on for this chapter.

-- 
Marc
